title: Bischler-Napieralski Isoquinoline Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Bischler-Napieralski isoquinoline synthesis describing cyclodehydration of acylated phenethylamines with dehydrating agents such as POCl3, P2O5, PPA, or Tf2O to give 3,4-dihydroisoquinolines or isoquinolines through intramolecular electrophilic aromatic substitution.
notes: Key concepts: cyclodehydration, acyliminium-like activation, isoquinoline assembly.

title: Bischler-Napieralski Isoquinoline Synthesis – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Bischler-Napieralski isoquinoline synthesis featuring annoretine, yohimbane/alloyohimbane, ancistrocladine, melinonine-E, and strychnoxanthine-type alkaloid frameworks assembled through isoquinoline-forming cyclization.

title: Brook Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Brook rearrangement describing intramolecular migration of a silyl group from carbon to oxygen under anionic conditions, including [1,2]- and [1,n]-silyl shifts through pentacoordinate silicon intermediates and the reverse retro-Brook process.
notes: Key concepts: acyl silanes, silyl migration, pentacoordinate silicon.

title: Brook Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Brook rearrangement featuring eight-membered carbocycle synthesis, the spirocyclic core of fredericamycin A, cyathin tricycles, and (+)-onocerin through Brook-mediated annulation, rearrangement/cyclization, or dimerization logic.
notes: Annulation and ring-expansion applications from acyl silanes.

title: Brown Hydroboration Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Brown hydroboration reaction describing regio- and stereoselective addition of borane across alkenes or alkynes to form organoboranes, typically with anti-Markovnikov selectivity, followed by oxidation or other transformations.
notes: Key concepts: BH3 addition, four-centered transition state, organoboranes.

title: Brown Hydroboration Reaction – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Brown hydroboration reaction featuring trinerine, cis-fused decalin synthesis, rhodium-catalyzed asymmetric hydroboration of styrenes, and the total synthesis of (-)-cassine via hydroboration/oxidation sequences.
notes: Hydroboration/oxidation and catalytic asymmetric hydroboration examples.

title: Buchner Method of Ring Expansion – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Buchner method of ring expansion describing carbene or metal-carbenoid addition to aromatic rings, formation of norcaradiene intermediates, and electrocyclic rearrangement to cycloheptatriene derivatives.
notes: Key concepts: diazo esters, rhodium carbenoids, norcaradiene/cycloheptatriene equilibrium.

title: Buchner Method of Ring Expansion – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Buchner method of ring expansion featuring azulene synthesis toward egualen sodium (KT1-32), fullerene functionalization, and harringtonolide construction through Buchner-mediated seven-membered ring formation.
notes: Ring-expansion and cycloheptatriene-based target synthesis examples.

title: Buchwald-Hartwig Cross-Coupling – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Buchwald-Hartwig cross-coupling describing palladium-catalyzed C-N and C-O bond formation between aryl halides or sulfonates and amines or alkoxides under basic conditions via oxidative addition, amido/alkoxo palladium formation, and reductive elimination.
notes: Key concepts: Pd catalysis, ligand-controlled amination/etherification.

title: Buchwald-Hartwig Cross-Coupling – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Buchwald-Hartwig cross-coupling featuring cyclazocine amine derivatives, 1,2-aziridinomitosene synthesis, and polysubstituted phenazines assembled by Pd-catalyzed aryl amination.
